title: Make replay ownership atomic
summary: Start with the database ownership contract, then verify worker integration, recovery coverage, and operator visibility.

chapters.title: Establish replay ownership
chapters.summary: The migration and claim query define uniqueness, lease expiry, and the atomic handoff between workers.

stops.title: Persist one active claim per delivery
stops.summary: Check the primary key and lease fields before reviewing the acquisition query.

stops.title: Acquire or recover in one statement
stops.summary: The conflict predicate is the concurrency boundary that prevents two workers from owning the same replay.

chapters.title: Carry ownership through delivery
chapters.summary: Verify the worker only delivers after receiving a claim and leaves a clear result for retries.

stops.title: Gate replay execution on ownership
stops.summary: The handler constructs the lease and exits when another worker owns the delivery.

stops.title: Expose active ownership to operators
stops.summary: The operations route distinguishes an actively claimed replay from an ordinary retry.

support.title: Concurrent worker regression coverage
support.reason: Confirms only one worker receives ownership while the lease remains active.

support.title: Operational recovery guidance
support.reason: Documents lease duration, rollout metrics, and manual recovery boundaries.
